'''Agnatius Paasi''' (born 30 November 1991) is a Tongan professional [[rugby league]] footballer who most recently played as a {{rlp|pr}} for [[St Helens RLFC|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]], and [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] at international level.

He previously played for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] in two separate spells, and also the [[Gold Coast Titans]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]]. Paasi also played for [[Salford RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a short-term loan from [[St Helens RLFC|St Helens]].

==Background==
Paasi was born in [[Lapaha, Tonga]], and moved to [[Auckland, New Zealand]] when he was three years old.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.taimionline.com/articles/3852|access-date=19 July 2014|url-status=usurped|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120602152315/http://www.taimionline.com/articles/3852|archive-date=2 June 2012|title=Young Warriors made in Tonga}}</ref>

Paasi played his junior football for [[Mangere East Hawks]]. At age 17, Paasi moved to Australia on a [[scholarship]] to play for [[Keebra Park State High School]] where they won the [[GIO Schoolboy Cup|Arrive Alive Cup]] in 2009. He also played some [[Australian rules football]] for the school.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.ourfootyteam.com/arrive_alive_cup_2009Archives.php |title=KEEBRA ARRIVE AS NEW CHAMPIONS |publisher=ourfootyteam.com |date=27 September 2009 |access-date=6 January 2016 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120805025211/http://www.ourfootyteam.com/arrive_alive_cup_2009Archives.php |archive-date=5 August 2012 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.ourfootyteam.com/keebraPark/keebra_park_2010_archives.php|title=KEEBRA FLY HIGH TO TAKE OUT GOLD COAST AFL TITLE|publisher=ourfootyteam.com|date=13 November 2009|access-date=6 January 2016}}</ref><ref name="Courier bio">{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/agnatius-paasis-incredible-journey-to-clinch-gold-coast-titans-debut/news-story/5256d2da254ff7c8a0689d643c33d59e|title=Agnatius Paasi's incredible journey to clinch Gold Coast Titans debut|publisher=The Courier Mail|date=6 March 2015|access-date=6 January 2016}}</ref> He was offered a contract with the [[Wests Tigers]] under 20's, but complications with his visa saw him return to New Zealand,<ref name="Courier bio"/> where he again played for the [[Mangere East Hawks]] before being signed by the [[New Zealand Warriors]].

==Playing career==
===Early career===
In 2011, Paasi played for the Warriors [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|Toyota Cup]] Under-20s team.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://photosportnz.photoshelter.com/image/I0000BQhl7RXwauo|title=Vodafone Junior Warriors Headshots, 2 March 2011 - Photosport New Zealand|publisher=}}</ref> On 5 October 2011, Paasi played in the Warriors' [[2011 NRL Under-20s season#Finals series|2011 NYC]] Grand Final victory over the [[North Queensland Cowboys]], playing off the interchange bench in the Warriors' 31-30 golden point extra time victory.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://leagueunlimited.com/news/teams-grand-final-day-2011/|title=TEAMS: Grand Final Day 2011|author=Tim Costello|work=League Unlimited}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/the-warriors-trump-the-north-queensland-cowboys-after-an-extra-time-field-goal-from-halfback-jordan-meads/story-e6freuy9-1226156482452|title=The Warriors Trump the North Queensland Cowboys After an Extra Time Field Goal from Halfback Jordan Meads|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2016-03-07}}</ref> On 6 October 2011, Paasi was named 18th man for the [[Junior Kiwis]].<ref>{{cite web | archive-url=https://archive.today/20140726224301/http://www.skysport.co.nz/article/league/headlines/junior-kiwis-field-huge-line-up-51003/ | archive-date=26 July 2014 | title=Junior Kiwis field huge line-up | publisher=SkySport.co.nz | date=16 October 2011 | url=http://www.skysport.co.nz/article/league/headlines/junior-kiwis-field-huge-line-up-51003/ | access-date=25 March 2018}}</ref> 
[[File:Agnatius Paasi.jpg|thumb|right|Paasi playing for the Auckland Vulcans in 2012]]
In 2012, Paasi moved on to the Warriors [[New South Wales Cup]] team, the [[Auckland Vulcans]], and he also played for the Vulcans in 2013. At the start of 2014 Paasi wasn't contracted to any NRL team but was given a chance to play in the Warriors pre-season games, earning a contract with the Warriors [[New South Wales Cup|NSW Cup]] side.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2014/07/19/paasis-persistence-pays-off/ |title=Paasi's persistence pays off |publisher=Warriors |author=Richard Becht  |date=19 July 2014 |access-date=27 October 2022}}</ref>

===2014===
After a few impressive performances in the NSW Cup, Paasi was handed his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] in Round 19 against the [[Brisbane Broncos]], where he played off the interchange bench in the Warriors 28–22 loss at [[Suncorp Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/gutsy-brisbane-broncos-fight-back-to-beat-new-zealand-warriors-in-thriller/news-story/3185985c7af90f31e0df4d7dc2e49b16|title=Gutsy Brisband Broncos Fight Back to Beat New Zealand Warriors in Thriller|website=Couriermail.com.au|access-date=2016-03-07}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/10269597/Thomas-Leuluai-back-from-injury-for-Warriors|title=Thomas Leuluai back from injury for Warriors|work=Stuff|date=15 July 2014 }}</ref> 
This was Paasi's only first grade match in the 2014 season. He was named as the Warriors NSW Cup Player of the Year.<ref>{{cite web|author=|url=http://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2014/09/19/agnatius_paasi_nsw_cup_player_of_the_year.html|title=Agnatius Paasi | NSW Cup award winner - Warriors|publisher=Warriors.kiwi|date=19 September 2014|access-date=2016-03-07}}</ref> In November, Paasi signed a train and trial contract with the [[Gold Coast Titans]], starting from 2015.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/paasis-puzzling-path-to-the-nrl/tabid/10874/newsid/83384/default.aspx|title=Paasi's puzzling path to the NRL|work=NRL - The official site of the National Rugby League - NRL.com|date=13 January 2015 }}</ref> On 16 October, Paasi played for [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]], starting at second-row in the 32–18 loss in [[Lae, Papua New Guinea|Lae]].<ref>{{cite web|author=|url=http://www.loverugbyleague.com/news_16551-png-beat-tonga-in-lae.html|title=PNG beat Tonga in Lae|website=Loverugbyleague.com|date=2014-10-19|access-date=2016-03-07}}</ref>

===2015===
In Round 1, Paasi made his club debut for the Gold Coast Titans against the [[Wests Tigers]], playing off the interchange bench in the Titans 19–18 loss at [[Cbus Super Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/agnatius-paasis-incredible-journey-to-clinch-gold-coast-titans-debut/news-story/5256d2da254ff7c8a0689d643c33d59e|title=Agnatius Paasi's Incredible Journey to Clinch Gold Coast Titans Debut|website=Couriermail.com.au|access-date=2016-03-07}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/high-shot-mars-wests-tigers-lastgasp-win-over-gold-coast-titans-20150307-13y0rh.html|title=High shot mars Wests Tigers' last-gasp win over Gold Coast Titans|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=7 March 2015 }}</ref> In Round 4 against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], he scored his first career try in the Titans 24–22 win at [[Remondis Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.theaustralian.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-titans-claim-first-win-of-season/news-story/28fd0268ecd0a40402a03258f8ec5e0c|title=NRL: Titans claim first win of season|work=The Australian}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/gold-coast-titans-re-sign-forward-pair/tabid/10874/newsid/87880/default.aspx |title=News Article not available |work=NRL - The official site of the National Rugby League - NRL.com |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150711011622/http://www.nrl.com/gold-coast-titans-re-sign-forward-pair/tabid/10874/newsid/87880/default.aspx |archive-date=2015-07-11 }}</ref> Paasi's good form in the Titans early matches earned him a 2-year contract extension with the Titans.<ref>{{cite web|author=|url=http://www.titans.com.au/news/2015/07/10/pettybourne_and_paas.html|title=Pettybourne and Paasi re-sign|website=Titans.com.au|date=9 July 2015|access-date=2016-01-06}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/gold-coast-titans-re-sign-forward-pair/tabid/10874/newsid/87880/default.aspx|access-date=19 July 2014|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150711011622/http://www.nrl.com/gold-coast-titans-re-sign-forward-pair/tabid/10874/newsid/87880/default.aspx|archive-date=11 July 2015|title= Gold Coast Titans re-sign forward pair|author=Ed Jackson}}</ref> He finished his impressive year with him playing in 18 matches and scoring 2 tries.

===2016===
In February, Paasi was named in the Titans [[2016 NRL Auckland Nines]] squad, where he was named in the Team of the Tournament. He was described as, "undoubtedly the weekend’s biggest surprise packet" and perhaps the best player of the Nines.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/titans/agnatius-paasi-leads-titans-to-semifinals-of-auckland-nines-in-most-breakout-performance/news-story/31699b4544fa50aa9ddd060c143a055d|title=Agnatius Paasi leads Titans to semi-finals of Auckland Nines in most breakout performance|work=Fox Sports|date=7 February 2016|access-date=2016-03-07|author=Nathan Tyan & Ben Glover}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.goldcoastbulletin.com.au/sport/nrl/gold-coast-forward-agnatius-paasi-close-to-player-of-tournament-in-titans-nines-semifinal-finish/news-story/49cd2774425086c74d48100981ee3e7a|title=Gold Coast Agnatius Paasi Close to Player of Tournament in Titans Nines Semifinal Finish|website=Goldcoastbulletin.com.au|access-date=2016-03-07}}</ref> Paasi enjoyed a solid [[2016 NRL season]] with him playing in all the Titans 25 matches and scored 5 tries.

===2017===
Paasi's [[2017 NRL season]] was limited due to a shoulder injury, playing in 11 matches for the Titans. On 5 November 2017, Paasi was released from his final year of his contract with the Titans to join his former club the [[New Zealand Warriors]] on a 2-year deal, starting from 2018.<ref>{{cite web| url = http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/titans/gold-coast-titans-to-grant-agnatius-paasi-immediate-release-on-compassionate-grounds/news-story/3c8748dadaacb2fb4826386ce9db7bdd| title = Reference at www.couriermail.com.au}}</ref><ref>{{cite web| url = https://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/98610555/warriors-confirm-signing-of-gold-coast-titans-forward-agnatius-paasi| title = Warriors confirm signing of Gold Coast Titans forward Agnatius Paasi {{!}} Stuff.co.nz| date = 6 November 2017}}</ref>

===2019===
In Round 24 2019, Paasi played his 100th NRL game<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2019/08/27/new-look-paasi-all-set-for-his-100th/ |title=New-look Paasi all set for his 100th |publisher=Warriors |date=27 August 2019 |access-date=27 October 2022}}</ref> in the Warriors 10–31 defeat by the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] at [[Mount Smart Stadium|Mt Smart Stadium]] in Auckland.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.theroar.com.au/rugby-league/live-score/new-zealand-warriors-vs-south-sydney-rabbitohs-nrl-live-scores-blog-816162/ |title=New Zealand Warriors vs South Sydney Rabbitohs: NRL match result, highlights |author=Scott Pryde |publisher=Roar |date=30 August 2019 |access-date=27 October 2022}}</ref>

===2020===
On 13 November, it was announced that Paasi had signed for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] on a two-year deal starting in 2021.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54935747|title=Agnatius Paasi: Tonga prop signs two-year St Helens deal|work=BBC Sport |access-date=15 Nov 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12164327/super-league-the-nrl-recruits-moving-to-these-shores-for-the-2021-season/|title=The NRL recruits moving to these shores for the 2021 season|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

===2021===
In round 1 of the 2021 Super League season, he made his debut for St. Helens in their 29–6 victory over Salford. Paasi was knocked out in the second half of the game after a collision with Salford player [[Pauli Pauli]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2021/mar/26/st-helens-salford-super-league-match-report-rugby-league/|title=Lomax leads way as St Helens make light work of Salford in season opener|newspaper=The Guardian|date=26 March 2021 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref> He was awarded Man of the Match on his Challenge Cup debut, spearheading his team's victory over Leeds.

On 17 July, he played for St. Helens in their 26-12 [[2021 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over Castleford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-celebrate-challenge-cup-21081897/|title=How St Helens players have celebrated their first Challenge Cup win in 13 years|website=www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk|access-date=18 July 2021|archive-date=18 July 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210718102540/https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-celebrate-challenge-cup-21081897|url-status=dead}}</ref>
On 9 October, he played for St. Helens in their [[2021 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58858850|title=Super League Grand Final: Catalans Dragons 10-12 St Helens: Saints win three in a row|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

===2022===
On 24 September, Paasi played for St Helens in their [[2022 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2022/sep/24/st-helens-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=St Helens sink Leeds to win record fourth consecutive Grand Final|newspaper=The Observer|date=24 September 2022 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref>

===2023===
On 18 February, Paasi played in St Helens 13–12 upset victory over [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]] in the [[2023 World Club Challenge]].<ref>{{cite news |title=St Helens stun Penrith to win World Club Challenge |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64666651 |work=BBC Sport}}</ref>
During St Helens loss to [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] in the Challenge Cup semi-final, Paasi suffered a knee injury due to a cannonball tackle from Leigh player [[John Asiata]]. It was confirmed that Paasi would be ruled out for an indefinite period as a result of the tackle. The following day, it was confirmed that Paasi would be ruled out for at least nine months.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/jon-wilkin-slams-incredibly-dangerous-tackles-as-st-helens-pair-suffer-serious-looking-injuries/|title=Jon Wilkin slams "incredibly dangerous" tackles as St Helens pair suffer serious-looking injuries|website=www.totalrl.com|date=23 July 2023 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/super-league-star-suffers-horrible-injury-78903/|title=Super League star suffers horrible injury|website=www.seriousaboutrl.com|date=24 July 2023 }}</ref>

===2024===
Paasi played 16 matches for St Helens in the 2024 Super League season which saw the club finish sixth on the table. He played in St Helens golden point extra-time playoff loss against Warrington.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2024/sep/28/warrington-st-helens-super-league-eliminator-playoff-rugby-league-match-report/|title=
'Soul destroying': Warrington eliminate St Helens in golden drop goal thriller|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

===2025===
Paasi played 25 games for St Helens in the 2025 Super League season including the clubs 20-12 semi-final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c04q6rqyrv6o/|title=Retiring Waerea-Hargreaves set to miss Grand Final|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===2026===
On 5 March 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Salford RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on short-term loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=5 Mar 2026|author=Matthew Shaw|title=Agnatius Paasi makes Championship move after continues St Helens omission|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/agnatius-paasi-championship-st-helens}}</ref>

On 25 May 2026 it was confirmed that he had left [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=25 May 2026|author=Aaron Bower|title=St Helens confirm exit of overseas forward to pave way for new signing|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/st-helens-confirm-exit-of-overseas-forward-to-pave-way-for-new-signing}}</ref>
